metadata.description: Fine-grained RBAC over an ENCRYPTED multi-tenant server (AES-256-GCM at rest): provision isolated tenant databases + roles/users/grants and assert the allow/deny matrix over REST and Bolt, with ciphertext-on-disk, offline key rotation and encrypted-backup proofs.

notes: storage.bytes_fsynced is a proxy: the WAL on-disk byte count (every committed WAL byte is fsynced before commit acknowledgement), not a directly-observed fsync counter.

RBAC allow/deny matrix asserted over BOTH REST and Bolt from one deterministic manifest; the hermetic security_verify proved ciphertext-on-disk (encrypted absent / cleartext present), offline key rotation (data intact, old key fails closed) and the encrypted backup roundtrip (no plaintext sealed, lossless restore).

Encryption overhead (enc_seed_ms vs clear_seed_ms, enc_store_bytes vs clear_store_bytes) is a coarse single-run measurement on shared hardware; the storage footprint delta is the stable signal (bounded per-page GCM tag/nonce overhead), the timing is machine-variant.
Live measurement of graphus-server pid 1352283 over 2.000s uptime; CPU is the server's cumulative since-boot usage (the process is dedicated to this workload), RSS is sampled from the live process.

COMMITTED BASELINE: the fast-profile reference run for the regression gate. Only the DETERMINISTIC structural metrics — dataset size and the on-disk store/WAL footprint — are gated (15% band) by sec_baseline_cmp; the machine-variant families (CPU, peak RSS, latency, throughput, and the single-run encryption-overhead timing) are NOT gated. Recorded on linux/x86_64.
